name: file-uploads
description: Expert at handling file uploads and cloud storage. Covers S3, Cloudflare R2, presigned URLs, multipart uploads, and image optimization. Knows how to handle large files without blocking. Use when: file upload, S3, R2, presigned URL, multipart.
capabilities: Generate presigned URLs for direct S3 and Cloudflare R2 uploads
Validate file types using magic bytes instead of file extensions
Implement multipart upload strategies for large files
Configure size limits and content-type restrictions
Sanitize filenames to prevent path traversal attacks
Optimize images during upload without blocking operations
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill is a documentation-only file (SKILL.md) providing guidance on secure file upload handling. Static analysis flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 26, but both are FALSE POSITIVES. Line 3 contains only a YAML description field, and line 26 is plain documentation text. No actual code execution, network calls, or cryptographic operations exist in this skill.
valueStatement: Upload files safely without blocking your application or exposing sensitive data. This skill provides proven patterns for presigned URLs, magic byte validation, and secure cloud storage integration.
userTitle: Handle File Uploads and Cloud Storage Securely
limitations: Does not execute file uploads directly - provides guidance only
Requires valid cloud storage credentials configured in your environment
Does not handle real-time upload progress monitoring
Limited to S3-compatible storage providers (S3, R2, MinIO)

useCases.title: Web application file upload feature
useCases.description: Build a secure image upload feature for a user profile system with automatic validation and cloud storage.

useCases.title: Large video file handling
useCases.description: Implement multipart uploads for video files exceeding 100MB without server memory issues.

useCases.title: Document management system
useCases.description: Create a secure document upload pipeline with type validation and organized cloud storage paths.

promptTemplates.title: Basic S3 Upload Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate code for uploading a user avatar image to S3 using a presigned URL. Validate the file is a valid image using magic bytes before allowing upload.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up basic file upload for user profiles

promptTemplates.title: Multipart Upload Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a multipart upload handler for files larger than 100MB. Include client-side chunking, progress tracking, and S3 multipart API integration.
promptTemplates.scenario: Handling large video or document uploads

promptTemplates.title: Secure Filename Sanitization
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a function that sanitizes uploaded filenames to prevent path traversal attacks. Remove dangerous characters, normalize unicode, and generate unique storage keys.

promptTemplates.title: Cloudflare R2 Migration
promptTemplates.prompt: Convert existing S3 upload code to work with Cloudflare R2. Include presigned URL generation, proper endpoint configuration, and cost optimization tips.
promptTemplates.scenario: Migrating from S3 to R2 for cost savings

bestPractices: Always validate file types using magic bytes, never trust file extensions or client-provided Content-Type headers
Use presigned URLs to let clients upload directly to cloud storage, avoiding server bandwidth costs
Set explicit size limits and enforce them both at the API gateway and in your upload handlers
antiPatterns: Storing uploaded files on the application server filesystem instead of cloud storage
Using the original filename as the storage key without sanitization
Proxying file uploads through your backend server instead of using presigned URLs

faq.question: What is a presigned URL and why should I use it?
faq.answer: A presigned URL is a time-limited URL that grants temporary access to upload or download from cloud storage. Using presigned URLs lets clients upload directly to S3 or R2, reducing server load and bandwidth costs while maintaining security through temporary credentials.

faq.question: How do I validate file types securely?
faq.answer: Check the file's magic bytes (header signature) instead of trusting the file extension or Content-Type header. For example, JPEG files start with bytes FF D8 FF. This prevents attackers from uploading malicious files with fake extensions.

faq.question: What size limits should I set for uploads?
faq.answer: Set limits based on your use case: profile images (5MB), documents (25MB), videos (500MB+). Always enforce limits at multiple layers: API gateway, application logic, and cloud storage bucket policies.

faq.question: How do I prevent path traversal attacks in filenames?
faq.answer: Never use the original filename directly. Strip all path components, remove or replace special characters, normalize unicode, and consider generating random storage keys while mapping original names in your database.

faq.question: What is multipart upload and when should I use it?
faq.answer: Multipart upload splits large files into chunks uploaded in parallel. Use it for files over 100MB. Benefits include: resumable uploads, parallel transfer speed, and ability to handle files up to 5TB in S3.

faq.question: Can I use this skill with storage providers other than S3?
faq.answer: Yes. The patterns work with any S3-compatible storage including Cloudflare R2, MinIO, DigitalOcean Spaces, and Wasabi. Only the endpoint URL and credentials need to change.
